title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
summary: Catie Cuan argues that the upcoming wave of robots should prioritize design for delight and human interaction rather than pursuing humanoid form factors and pure utility. She critiques the humanoid robot race led by companies like 1X and Unitree as a myopic vision, suggesting this narrow focus misses broader opportunities for robot design and societal integration.
writeup: Catie Cuan of Stanford argues that the coming wave of robotics, framed here as a "ChatGPT moment," will hinge less on raw intelligence and more on design for delight and human interaction. She critiques the humanoid robot race led by companies such as 1X and Unitree as a myopic vision, contending that fixation on humanoid form factors and pure utility crowds out broader possibilities for robot design and societal integration. The takeaway: the real constraint is how robots fit into human contexts, not how capable their underlying models become.
